name: ElmAdmin

aliases.name: RouteParams
aliases.type: { path : String.String, pathParams : Dict.Dict String.String String.String, queryParams : Dict.Dict String.String (List.List String.String) }

values.name: admin
values.comment:
Bootstraps your admin application.

    admin
        { title = "My Admin"
        , init = init
        }
        [ theme [ preferDarkMode ]
        , update myUpdate
        , pages
            [ A.external "Docs" "https://package.elm-lang.org/"
            , A.single "Home" Home.page
            , A.group "Users"
                Users.index
                [ A.single "Create" Users.create
                , A.url Users.show
                , A.url Users.update
                ]
            ]
        ]
values.type: { title : String.String, init : flags -> Browser.Navigation.Key -> ( model, ElmAdmin.Shared.Action msg ) } -> List.List (ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg) -> ElmAdmin.ElmAdmin flags model msg

values.name: darkModeClass
values.comment:
Sets the dark mode strategy. Check out [elm-theme-spec](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/uncover-co/elm-theme-spec/latest/) for more.

Tip: If you're using tailwind's `dark:` variants you might want to set this to `ThemeSpec.ClassStrategy "dark"`.
values.type: ThemeSpec.DarkModeStrategy -> ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions -> ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions

values.name: darkTheme
values.comment: Sets the theme used on dark mode.
values.type: ThemeSpec.Theme -> ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions -> ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions

values.name: disableModeSwitch
values.comment: Prevents the user from selecting a dark or light mode.
values.type: ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions -> ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions

values.name: disabled
values.comment:
Conditionally disable nav links and their pages. They won't be accessible even through urls.

    single "Roles" Roles.page
        |> disable (\_ model -> not (userIsAdmin model))
values.type: (ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Basics.Bool) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: external
values.comment:
Used for creating external links in your sidebar.

    external "Docs" "https://package.elm-lang.org/"
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: folderGroup
values.comment:
A group that shows its items only if one of them is the current active path.

    folderGroup "Workspaces"
        Workspace.index
        [ single "New" Workspaces.new
        , url "Show" Workspaces.show
        , url "Update" Workspaces.update
        ]
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> ElmAdmin.Internal.Page.Page model msg params -> List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: group
values.comment:
Used for creating grouped pages. Note that the "group" is also a page and if it is hidden or disabled by any means, then the whole group will follow.

    group "Workspace"
        Workspace.index
        [ single "New" Workspace.create
        , hidden "Update" Workspace.update
        ]
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> ElmAdmin.Internal.Page.Page model msg params -> List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: hidden
values.comment:
Conditionally hide nav links.

    single "Roles" Roles.page
        |> hidden (\_ model -> not (userIsAdmin model))
values.type: (ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Basics.Bool) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: lightTheme
values.comment: Sets the theme used on light mode.
values.type: ThemeSpec.Theme -> ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions -> ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions

values.name: pages
values.comment: Defines a list of pages for your application.
values.type: List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg

values.name: preferDarkMode
values.comment: Starts the admin on dark mode.
values.type: ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions -> ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions

values.name: protectedPages
values.comment: Defines a list of pages for your application.
values.type: { fromModel : model -> Maybe.Maybe protectedModel, toModel : model -> protectedModel -> model } -> List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem protectedModel msg) -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg

values.name: single
values.comment:
Used for creating a single page with a nav link.

    single "/users/:userId" "User" User.page

Note that all page links with params will only appear if they are already present on the current page path.

    -- User.page { path = "/users/:userId", ... }

    single "User" User.page

    -- This page link will appear if the user is on "/settings/:userId"
    -- But if the user is on "/", no ":userId" is available so the link will disappear.
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> ElmAdmin.Internal.Page.Page model msg params -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: subscriptions
values.type: (ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg) -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg

values.name: theme
values.comment: Customize your admin theme through a list of options.
values.type: List.List (ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions -> ElmAdmin.ThemeOptions) -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg

values.name: update
values.type: (ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> msg -> model -> ( model, ElmAdmin.Shared.Action msg )) -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg -> ElmAdmin.Options flags model protectedModel msg

values.name: url
values.comment:
Used for creating pages that will not appear as nav links but are still accessible through their direct url.

    url Home.page
values.type: String.String -> ElmAdmin.Internal.Page.Page model msg params -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: visualGroup
values.comment:
Creates a visual group (The group label itself is not a nav link).

Tip: This can be useful if you want to disable whole sections of your application but you don't want that to be related to one specific root page.

    visualGroup "Workspace"
        [ single "New" Workspace.create
        , hidden "Update" Workspace.update
        ]
values.type: String.String -> List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

name: ElmAdmin.Actions
comment:
Actions are a especial kind of `Cmd` that can be used to trigger both your usual commands as well as especial ones used by ElmAdmin.

You can use commands that will be handled by your own update functions.

    import ElmAdmin.Actions as Actions

    update : RouteParams -> Msg -> Model -> ( Model, Action Msg )
    update _ msg model =
        case msg of
            RequestedUsers ->
                ( model
                , Actions.cmd <| fetchUsers GotUsers
                )

            GotUsers users ->
                ( { model | users = users }
                , Actions.none
                )

But you can also trigger actions that will be handled by ElmAdmin itself.

    import ElmAdmin.Actions as Actions

    update : RouteParams -> Msg -> Model -> ( Model, Action Msg )
    update _ msg model =
        case msg of
            RequestedUsers ->
                ( model
                , Actions.cmd <| fetchUsers GotUsers
                )

            GotUsers users ->
                ( { model | users = users }
                , Actions.showNotification <|
                    text "Got some users!"
                )


# Commands

@docs cmd, none, batch, Action


# Notifications

You can trigger a notification popup from anywhere in your application through one of these commands.

@docs showNotification, showHighlightNotification, showSuccessNotification, showWarningNotification, showDangerNotification


# Form

@docs initForm

values.name: batch
values.comment: Batchs a list of actions. You can mix and match your cmds and ElmAdmin actions by using this function.
values.type: List.List (ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg) -> ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg

values.name: cmd
values.comment: Sends a command that will be handled by your update functions.
values.type: Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg -> ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg

values.name: initForm
values.type: ElmAdmin.Form.Form resource -> resource -> ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg

values.name: none
values.comment: Does nothing. This is similar to `Cmd.none`.
values.type: ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg

values.name: showDangerNotification
values.comment: Triggers a notification with a "danger" theme.
values.type: Html.Html msg -> ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg

values.name: showHighlightNotification
values.comment: Triggers a notification with a "highlight" theme.
values.type: Html.Html msg -> ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg

values.name: showNotification
values.comment: Triggers a notification that will fade out after a while.
values.type: Html.Html msg -> ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg

values.name: showSuccessNotification
values.comment: Triggers a notification with a "success" theme.
values.type: Html.Html msg -> ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg

values.name: showWarningNotification
values.comment: Triggers a notification with a "warning" theme.
values.type: Html.Html msg -> ElmAdmin.Actions.Action msg
